Every move felt aggressive, unpredictable, like he was constantly fighting something inside himself. The energy wasnât just physicalâit was emotional, explosive, and honestly a little dangerous.

Now? Itâs quieterâ¦ but not softer. The shift is clean, almost surgical. Bright spaces, glass walls, composed body language. Heâs not trying to prove anything anymoreâhe just exists in control. The chaos didnât disappear, it evolved. You can still feel it under the surface, just more refined, more calculated. That calm demeanor? It feels intentional, not natural. Like he learned how to hide the storm instead of stopping it.

Thatâs what makes this evolution so interesting. Itâs not about becoming betterâitâs about becoming different. Same person, different mask. And somehow, that version might be even more dangerous than before.

The Summer I Turned Pretty is an American coming-of-age romantic drama television series created by author Jenny Han for Amazon Prime Video. It is based on her novel trilogy: The Summer I Turned Pretty, It's Not Summer Without You, and We'll Always Have Summer. Lola Tung stars as Belly Conklin, a teenager involved in a love triangle with brothers Conrad and Jeremiah, played by Christopher Briney and Gavin Casalegno, respectively.

Production on the TV series began in 2021. It premiered on June 17, 2022, with the first season consisting of seven episodes. Before its premiere, the series was renewed for a second season, which debuted on July 14, 2023, and includes eight episodes. In August 2023, the series was renewed for a third season. The 11-episode third and final season premiered on July 16, 2025. A feature-length film was announced following the series conclusion on September 17, 2025, and is set to serve as a direct continuation of the series finale.

share.share_text: Conrad Fisherâs love for Belly Conklin in The Summer I Turned Pretty series is a deep, enduring, and often quietly intense affection rooted in years of shared childhood summers at Cousins Beach. Initially viewing her as a little sister-like figure, Conrad gradually develops romantic feelings as Belly grows into a young woman, though he struggles to express them openly due to his moody, guarded nature and personal hardships like family issues and his motherâs illness. His love manifests through protective actions, subtle gestures, and sacrifices rather than grand declarationsâhe silently supports her, pushes her away at times to shield her from his pain, yet consistently shows he cares deeply, making her laugh, comforting her in vulnerable moments, and ultimately confessing that she is the only girl he could ever truly be with. Even after breakups, heartbreaks, and her relationship with his brother Jeremiah, Conradâs feelings persist unwaveringly, culminating in heartfelt admissions that he never stopped loving her, proving his devotion is profound, lifelong, and tied to their unbreakable history together.

The Summer I Turned Pretty Season 3 (2025) follows Belly in her junior year of college, balancing a seemingly stable relationship with Jeremiah in Cousins Beach with unresolved feelings for Conrad. Stuffed Mango Kulfi or Saffron Mango Kulfi 

Recipe
1. Prepare the Mango â Cut off the top of a ripe mango and carefully remove the seed, keeping the mango intact.
2. Make the Kulfi Mixture â Boil full-fat milk, add condensed milk, and cook until it reduces to half. Stir in Kesar and sugar, cooking until the mixture thickens. Optionally, add dry fruits.
3. Fill the Mango â Let the mixture cool slightly, then pour it into the hollowed mango.
4. Freeze â Place the mango in a container with crushed ice and salt. Let it set for 6-8 hours or freeze for a firmer texture.
5. Serve â Peel the mango, slice it into rounds, garnish, and enjoy!

Mango Mastani 

Recipe:

Take peeled mango and blend it with milk, condensed milk, and vanilla ice cream until smooth. Add some mango chunks and mix lightly. Keep this mixture in the fridge for half a day for better taste.

For serving, make a milkshake by blending mango, vanilla ice cream, and milk. Pour the milkshake into a glass, add the prepared mango mixture on top, then scoop extra vanilla ice cream. Garnish with mango pieces and dry fruits. Serve chilled and enjoy.

Few artists have shaped modern music and global pop culture as profoundly as Michael Jackson and Kanye West, each redefining their era through innovation, controversy, and cultural influence. Emerging in the late 20th century, Jackson rose from his early success with the Jackson 5 to become the âKing of Pop,â setting unprecedented commercial and artistic benchmarks with albums like Thriller (1982), which remains the best-selling album of all time. His groundbreaking music videos, including those for âBillie Jeanâ and âBeat It,â transformed MTV into a dominant force and elevated the visual dimension of music into a core part of the industry.

Decades later, Kanye West would similarly disrupt the musical landscape, first gaining recognition as a producer before redefining hip-hop with his debut album The College Dropout (2004). Known for blending genres, introspective lyricism, and bold artistic risks, West expanded the boundaries of rap with projects like 808s & Heartbreak and My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy. While both artists achieved critical and commercial success, they also became polarizing figures, navigating intense media scrutiny and public controversy. Together, Jackson and West represent two distinct generations of musical innovationâone pioneering the global pop blueprint, the other reshaping hip-hop and modern productionâeach leaving a lasting imprint on how music is created, performed, and consumed worldwide. #MichaelJackson #KanyeWest #MusicHistory #PopCulture #HipHop

January 28, 1986. Seventy-three seconds after launch, the Space Shuttle Challenger disintegrated over the Atlantic.
It was meant to be routine. The shuttle program had begun to feel almost operational â launches televised in classrooms, astronauts framed as professionals rather than pioneers. Challenger carried seven crew members, including Christa McAuliffe, a schoolteacher selected to demonstrate that space was no longer reserved for test pilots.
The temperature that morning in Florida was unusually cold.
Engineers at Morton Thiokol, the contractor responsible for the shuttleâs solid rocket boosters, had warned about the risk. The boosters relied on rubber O-rings to seal joints between segments. In low temperatures, the material became less elastic. The night before launch, engineers recommended delay.
Management overruled them.
When Challenger lifted off, hot gases began leaking past a compromised O-ring on the right booster. For just over a minute, the shuttle appeared stable. Then a plume of flame breached the external fuel tank. The vehicle broke apart under aerodynamic stress.
There was no explosion in the cinematic sense. The shuttle was torn apart by forces it was never designed to endure.
The disaster halted the shuttle program for nearly three years. A presidential commission, including physicist Richard Feynman, investigated the cause. In a now-famous demonstration, Feynman placed a piece of O-ring material in ice water during a hearing and showed how it lost resilience in the cold.
The failure was not mysterious. It was institutional.
Warning signs had existed. Data had been debated. Schedule pressure â political, financial, symbolic â had grown. Risk had been normalised because previous flights had survived similar erosion.
Challenger exposed the gap between engineering reality and organisational optimism.
Space is unforgiving. It does not negotiate with deadlines, public relations, or budget cycles.
73 seconds was all it took to relearn that.

âThe "Sherlock Holmes" Vibe: The episode features a sequence where Peter uses his new intellect to solve mundane problems with high-level deduction, parodying modern depictions of Sherlock Holmes (like the BBC series Sherlock).

âLoisâs Reaction: Ironically, while Lois wanted Peter to be smarter, she quickly realizes she hates the "new" Peter because he is arrogant and looks down on her, leading her to try and "de-program" him back to his old, dim-witted self.

Monster House is a 2006 American computer-animated supernatural horror comedy film directed by Gil Kenan in his feature debut. Produced by ImageMovers (Robert Zemeckis) and Amblin Entertainment (Steven Spielberg), with a screenplay by Dan Harmon, Rob Schrab, and Pamela Pettler, it runs about 1 hour 31 minutes and is rated PG for scary images/sequences, brief language, some crude humor, and thematic elements.

This spooky, inventive kidsâ adventure blends childhood fears, suburban Halloween vibes, and clever animation (using motion-capture for performances). It has a cult following for its creepy-yet-fun tone that appeals to both children and nostalgic adults.

Quick Spoiler-Free Plot Summary

In a quiet suburban neighborhood, 12-year-old D.J. Walters (Mitchel Musso) has long suspected something sinister about the dilapidated house across the street owned by the grouchy old man Horace Nebbercracker (Steve Buscemi). When Nebbercracker has a medical emergency and is taken away, strange things start happening â toys and objects that land on the lawn get swallowed up, and the house itself seemsâ¦ alive.

As Halloween approaches, D.J. teams up with his best friend Chowder (Sam Lerner) and a smart, no-nonsense girl named Jenny (Spencer Locke) to investigate. They soon discover the terrifying truth: the house is a living, breathing monster with a dark secret tied to its past. The trio must find a way to stop it before it devours the entire neighborhood â especially trick-or-treaters on Halloween night. Expect clever traps, wild chases, gross-out gags, and a surprisingly touching backstory.

Interesting fact:

he engineers who find the best roles fastest are almost never the ones who mass applied to a hundred job postings on LinkedIn and waited, because the public job board is the most competitive and least efficient channel in the entire hiring ecosystem, representing the opportunities that every other hiring method failed to fill first. The highest conversion rate path into a good engineering role runs almost entirely through warm introductions, where a former colleague, a conference connection, or even a thoughtful cold message to someone whose work you genuinely admire can bypass the applicant tracking system entirely and land your resume directly in front of a hiring manager who is now predisposed to give you the benefit of the doubt before reading a single line. The engineers who have mastered job searching treat it like a product launch with a deliberate strategy: they update their LinkedIn to signal passive availability without broadcasting desperation, they reach out to their network before they need anything so the relationship is warm when they do, they research target companies deeply enough to ask questions in interviews that make it obvious they did their homework, and they time their search to avoid the January flooding when every engineer who made a New Year's resolution to switch jobs hits the market simultaneously. The uncomfortable truth about job searching in tech is that the quality of your network is a better predictor of your next offer than the quality of your resume, which means the best time to invest in professional relationships is years before you need them.

When did you last genuinely invest in a professional relationship with no immediate job search agenda attached to it?
